quiz.title: Unit 2 Quiz 9, Grade 10 Biology
quiz.subtitle: Ecology: Ecosystem Types & Earth's Spheres
quiz.subject: Biology
quiz.topic: Terrestrial vs. Aquatic Ecosystems, Decomposers, Artificial Ecosystems, Earth's Spheres

questions.question: Which one of the following is not a sub-category of terrestrial ecosystem?
questions.explanation: Marine ecosystem is an aquatic ecosystem, not terrestrial. Terrestrial ecosystems are land-based and include mountain, lowland, forest, grassland, desert, and semi-arid ecosystems.

questions.question: Identify an example of decomposers that are found in the bottom of a pond.
questions.explanation: Flagellates (certain protozoa and bacteria) are decomposers found at pond bottoms, breaking down organic matter. Zooplankton and phytoplankton are consumers/producers, and jellyfish are not typically decomposers.

questions.question: Which of the following can be classified as an artificial ecosystem?
questions.explanation: Gardens are artificial ecosystems created and maintained by humans. Forests, lakes, and ponds are natural ecosystems, though ponds can sometimes be artificial depending on origin.

questions.question: Which statement about the water cycle is FALSE?
options: Evaporation moves water from surface reservoirs to the atmosphere.
Transpiration by plants contributes to atmospheric moisture.
Precipitation occurs when water vapor absorbs heat from the clouds.
Runoff transports water and sediments to rivers, lakes, and oceans.
questions.explanation: Precipitation results from condensation when water vapor cools and forms droplets heavy enough to fall. It does not occur because water vapor absorbs heat; absorption of heat would cause evaporation, not precipitation.

questions.question: The Lithosphere represents _______________
options: the water layer on Earth's surface
the solid outer layer of Earth
questions.explanation: The lithosphere is the solid outer layer of Earth, including the crust and upper mantle. It consists of rocks, minerals, and soil.

questions.question: The Hydrosphere represents _______________
options: the solid rock layer of Earth
the layer of gases surrounding Earth
questions.explanation: The hydrosphere includes all water on Earth — oceans, seas, lakes, rivers, groundwater, glaciers, and water vapor in the atmosphere.

questions.question: The Atmosphere represents _______________
options: the solid Earth beneath our feet
the layer of gases surrounding Earth
all bodies of water on Earth
questions.explanation: The atmosphere is the gaseous envelope surrounding Earth, composed mainly of nitrogen, oxygen, carbon dioxide, and other gases.

questions.question: Which of the following is a marine organism?
questions.explanation: Seaweed (algae) is a marine organism living in saltwater environments. Earthworms live in soil, cacti are desert plants, and mushrooms are fungi typically found on land.

questions.question: Which of the following is an arboreal organism?
questions.explanation: Arboreal organisms live primarily in trees. Squirrels are classic examples. Whales are aquatic, rabbits are terrestrial burrowers, and earthworms live in soil.

questions.question: In which sphere would you find clouds and weather patterns?
questions.explanation: Clouds and weather patterns occur in the atmosphere, specifically the troposphere, which is the lowest layer of Earth's gaseous envelope.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You understand ecosystem classification and Earth's spheres clearly.
scoreMessages.70: Good job. Review the differences between terrestrial and aquatic ecosystems.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on the four Earth spheres and their characteristics.
scoreMessages.0: Please review ecosystem types, decomposer examples, and Earth's physical systems.
